display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page. Archivo at the expanded end of its width axis (wdth 118).

headline.purpose: Section titles on the landing route and the console page title.

title.purpose: The live stage name over the reconstruction canvas.

body.purpose: Running prose at 18px, capped at 60ch (renders ~71 characters in Archivo).

label.purpose: Nav, field names, panel headings, buttons. Archivo condensed (wdth 62), uppercase, 0.3em tracking.

data.purpose: Every measured value, endpoint signature, identifier and margin annotation. JetBrains Mono, tabular-nums.

motion.name: control
motion.purpose: Button fill and text colour swap on hover.

motion.name: marker-state
motion.purpose: Pipeline stage marker changing colour as a stage becomes ready, busy, done or failed.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: GSAP section reveal as a band enters the viewport. Suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion via gsap.matchMedia.

motion.name: scrub
motion.purpose: Binds the reconstruction canvas to scroll position rather than a timer. Under reduced motion the canvas paints its final state and the 560vh track collapses to 100vh.

motion.name: pulse
motion.purpose: Busy stage marker. Disabled entirely under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The landing route CTA. Graphite fill, square, inverts to Blueprint Indigo on hover.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Console stage action. Transparent until hover, when fill and text swap. Disabled state is visibly inert, not dimmed-and-hopeful.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Console stage input with its condensed uppercase label above.
components.html: <label class="ds-field">Scan id<input class="ds-input" placeholder="from the phone app"></label>

components.name: Panel
components.description: The only container in the system. Deep Vellum on vellum, 1.5px rule, square, no shadow. Never nested.
components.html:
Jobs

Waiting for the first job — run a stage.

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Masthead nav shared by both routes. The active item takes Graphite text plus a Signal Orange Ink underline, so colour is never the sole signal.

components.name: Stage Marker
components.description: Pipeline rail marker. One of only two circles in the system, because it represents a point in a sequence rather than a container. Colour and border together encode ready / busy / done / failed.
components.html: 01020304

components.name: Code Block
components.description: The SDK sample. The one dark surface in the system: graphite ground, square, no shadow.
components.html:
from dragverse import DragVerse

tf = DragVerse("http://localhost:8000")

narrative.overview:
Everything on screen is one drawing, trimmed to register. The page is a large-format survey sheet: corner registration marks, a marginalia column carrying field annotations in monospace, sheet numbering in the masthead (SHEET 01 / 02), and hairline rules dividing the work. Nothing floats above the sheet, because a drawing has no drop shadow. The landing route and the operator console are two sheets of the same document, sharing a masthead and a token set, so moving between them never feels like a handoff to another team.

The colour comes from the product itself rather than from a mood board. A depth sensor returns a ramp — near is hot, far is cool — and that ramp is the brand. Signal Orange appears only where the system is sensing or working right now; Survey Teal marks what has been labelled; Blueprint Indigo carries structure and finished artifacts. Colour is state, never decoration. When a value has not been measured, the interface says so in words rather than showing a zero.

This system explicitly rejects the dark neon robotics site — near-black with acid green or cyan, glowing grids, sci-fi HUD chrome — which is the reflex default for anything robot-adjacent and reads as costume rather than instrument. It equally rejects the generic AI-SaaS landing and, above all, fabricated precision: invented benchmarks or decorative metrics that no code produced.
keyCharacteristics: Absolutely flat. Zero box-shadow declarations in the entire system.
Square by default. Zero border-radius except two deliberate circles.
Hairlines at 1.5px, never 1px — the viewing context is a projector in a bright room.
One variable typeface worked at both ends of its width axis, plus one monospace.
Colour encodes pipeline state; it is never applied for visual interest.

rules.name: The Hot Ink Rule
rules.body: Signal Orange (#ff5b2e) and Survey Teal (#14b09a) are graphic colours only. On vellum they measure 2.69:1 and 3.49:1 and fail as text. Any type, icon-with-label, or state indicator that must be read uses the -ink counterpart. Audit test: if you can read words in it, it must be an -ink value.

rules.name: The Measured Colour Rule
rules.body: Colour reports pipeline state and nothing else. Orange means happening now. Teal means identified. Indigo means settled. Red means refused. A colour applied because a section needed some life is prohibited.

rules.name: The 14px Floor Rule
rules.body: No text anywhere renders below 0.875rem/14px. Body copy is 16px or larger. The primary viewing context is a projector in a bright room with a back row. Font sizes are always rem, never px.

rules.name: The Two-Ramp Rule
rules.body: Sizes come from exactly two scales and are never authored ad hoc. --ui-* is a fixed rem scale (14/16/18px) for the console. --brand-* is a fluid clamp() scale for the landing, with >=1.25 between heading steps. A raw value in a font-size declaration is a bug.

rules.name: The One Kicker Rule
rules.body: A small uppercase tracked label above a section heading is permitted once per route, as voice. Repeating it above every section is scaffolding, not design.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces separate by tone and hairline, never by shadow. Audit test: if it casts a shadow, it is not on the sheet.

rules.name: The Square Rule
rules.body: border-radius is 0 unless the element represents a literal point in space, in which case it is a full circle. There is no middle. A 12px rounded card does not exist in this system.

dos: Do use -ink colour variants for anything readable: #a82c0c and #06584e. The saturated #ff5b2e and #14b09a are graphics-only.
Do keep every hairline at var(--hair) (1.5px). 1px disappears on a projector.
Do pull every font size from --ui-* or --brand-*. Never author a raw value.
Do state absence explicitly. "Not profiled yet" with the command that fixes it, never a zero or an empty chart.
Do give every scroll-driven or timeline animation a prefers-reduced-motion equivalent that conveys the same information, and collapse any tall scroll track that becomes purposeless without the scrub.
Do separate surfaces with tone (#eef0ea to #e6e9e1) and hairlines.
Do cap running prose at 60ch and use text-wrap: balance on headings, pretty on prose.
donts: Don't build the dark neon robotics site — near-black with acid green or cyan, glowing grids, sci-fi HUD chrome. Named in PRODUCT.md as the anti-reference.
Don't build the generic AI-SaaS landing: gradient hero metric, three identical feature cards, a tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't ship fabricated precision — invented benchmark numbers, decorative metrics, or a populated-looking dashboard no code produced.
Don't add a box-shadow. Anywhere. If it casts a shadow, it is not on the sheet.
Don't add border-radius between 0 and 50%. There is no 12px card in this system.
Don't put a coloured stripe on one edge of a card or callout via border-left.
Don't render text below 14px, and don't set font sizes in px.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text), glassmorphism, or a blur as decoration.
Don't repeat a small uppercase kicker above every section. One per route.
Don't number sections 01 / 02 / 03 unless the content genuinely is an ordered sequence.
Don't let colour be the sole carrier of state. Pair it with text, weight, or shape.
